title: CSCI 585 — Database Systems (USC, Spring 2023)
description: Two interactive demos from Yan Liu's CSCI 585 alongside the original homework source. The Bay Area photo spatial demo plots 13 geotagged photos on a real map and runs convex-hull / k-NN queries client-side via turf.js. The YouTube SQL demo runs the original 7-table relational schema on PGlite (real PostgreSQL compiled to WebAssembly), letting you execute the 6 query exercises live in the browser. Original homework source lives alongside in HWs/HW{1..5}/.

assets.title: HW3 — Bay Area Photo Spatial Queries
assets.description: 13 geotagged photos around Fremont/Pleasanton/Livermore on a maplibre-gl basemap. Toggle convex hull, click anywhere to find the 4 nearest photos, enable density to see a Gaussian kernel-density visualization. Same dataset as the original PostGIS HW3, computed client-side via turf.js (no server-side DB required).

assets.title: HW2 — YouTube SQL Schema (PGlite)
assets.description: The 7-table YouTube-like relational schema running on real PostgreSQL via PGlite (WASM). Boots with a small synthetic dataset, then a sidebar button lets you replay the original course `create_table.sql` + `insert_data.sql` — the same SQL submitted for graded HW2 (1000 users, ~150K comments). Either mode runs the 6 query exercises live; or browse the original course source files (q1.sql … q6.sql, the literal graded answers) in the sidebar's 'Course source files' section. Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to execute.
